title: 9 Herbal Creams For Hyperacidity
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great way to soothe and calm hyperacidity. But what about herbal teas? They can be just as effective, if not more so. The reason is that many herbs have natural anti-inflammatory and antacid properties that can help neutralize excess stomach acid. For example, Curcuma longa (turmeric) contains a powerful compound called curcumin that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. Similarly, Zingiber officinale (ginger) has natural antacid properties that can help calm the stomach and reduce heartburn. And Glycyrrhiza glabra (licorice root) has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues, including hyperacidity. When you drink herbal teas made from these herbs, the active compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can start to work their magic. This can bring a range of benefits, from reducing digestive discomfort and bloating, to improving your overall well-being and energy levels. Many people find that drinking herbal teas regularly can help them feel more relaxed and calm, and even reduce stress and anxiety. Plus, they're often gentle and easy to digest, making them a great option for people who are sensitive to medication or have other health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams contains curcumin, demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize excess acid in the stomach. Curcumin's ability to inhibit the production of stomach acid and its anti-inflammatory effects make it a valuable ingredient in managing hyperacidity. Demethoxycurcumin and bisdemethoxycurcumin also contribute to this effect by reducing inflammation in the stomach lining and promoting healing of the mucous membrane. By reducing inflammation and neutralizing excess acid, Curcuma longa creams can provide relief from symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and acid reflux.
remedies.image_alt: Curcuma longa herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in the double boiler over low heat.
Add the turmeric powder to the melted mixture and stir well.
Add the beeswax to the mixture and stir until it's fully incorporated and melted.
Remove the mixture from heat, let it cool, and then pour it into a container. Let it solidify before using it as a cream for hyperacidity relief.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contains bioactive constituents such as gingerol and shogaol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and neutralize free radicals in the body. Gingerols and shogaols also have a natural digestive enzyme-inhibiting effect, which helps to reduce the production of stomach acid and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. Additionally, Zingiber officinale creams contain a compound called curcumin, which has a soothing effect on the digestive tract and helps to calm down acid production. The combined effects of these bioactive constituents make Zingiber officinale creams a useful treatment for hyperacidity.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a bowl.
Heat the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water until melted.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried and powdered Zingiber officinale (ginger) root to the melted mixture and stir well.
Add the distilled water to the mixture and stir until combined. Remove from heat.
Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool and solidify. Once solid, your Zingiber officinale cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains active constituents such as glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, which is a common cause of hyperacidity. Flavonoids and saponins have antioxidant properties that protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive acid production. Glycyrrhizin also helps to reduce the production of stomach acid, which can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these constituents work together to provide relief from hyperacidity and soothe the stomach lining.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water and 1 tablespoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder in a saucepan.
Boil the water and add the Glycyrrhiza glabra root powder. Let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a bowl and let it cool. Add 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax melts. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool and solidify. Use as a cream to soothe hyperacidity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains the bioactive constituents menthone, menthyl acetate, and limonene. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce acidity in the stomach. Menthol, a key component, has a cooling effect that slows down the digestion of food, giving the stomach a chance to heal. Additionally, menthone and limonene have antioxidant properties that protect the stomach lining from damage caused by excessive acidity. When applied topically as a cream, these properties can help soothe and calm the stomach, providing relief from hyperacidity symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of distilled water, 2 cups of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of Mentha x piperita oil.
Combine the distilled water and Mentha x piperita oil in a bowl. Stir well to mix.
In a separate bowl, mix the coconut oil and beeswax. Heat the mixture in a double boiler until the beeswax is melted.
Slowly add the water and Mentha x piperita mixture to the coconut oil mixture. Stir well to mix.
Pour the mixture into a container and let it cool. Once cooled, the Mentha x piperita cream is ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum creams contains essential oils, including cinnamaldehyde, linalool, and eugenol. These bioactive constituents have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe the digestive system. Cinnamaldehyde, in particular, has been shown to slow down the movement of food from the stomach to the small intestine, reducing the symptoms of hyperacidity. Linalool, on the other hand, has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate stress and anxiety that may be contributing to hyperacidity. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Cinnamomum verum creams may provide relief from the discomfort associated with hyperacidity.
remedies.image_alt: Cinnamomum verum herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cinnamomum verum bark, 2 cups of coconut oil, and 1 cup of beeswax.
Dry the Cinnamomum verum bark in a low-temperature oven (150°F - 200°F) for 2 hours.
Grind the dried Cinnamomum verum bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler, then add 2 tablespoons of the Cinnamomum verum powder.
Mix well and pour the mixture into a container, let it cool and harden before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams contains active constituents such as fenchone, anethole, and limonene, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Fenchone has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive tract, reducing inflammation caused by hyperacidity. Anethole, on the other hand, has antispasmodic properties that help relax the muscles in the digestive tract, preventing acid reflux. Limonene, a terpene, has antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria that can contribute to hyperacidity. By using Foeniculum vulgare creams, individuals can potentially alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity and promote a healthy digestive system.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Foeniculum vulgare, 2 cups of coconut oil, and 1 cup of shea butter.
Grind the dried Foeniculum vulgare into a fine powder using a blender or spice grinder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the ground Foeniculum vulgare powder with 8 tablespoons of coconut oil and 4 tablespoons of shea butter in a saucepan.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, until the shea butter melts and the mixture is smooth.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and let the mixture cool. Once cooled, transfer it to a jar and store it in the refrigerator.

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum creams contains a compound called piperine, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. The boiactive constituents of Piper nigrum creams, including piperine, terpenes, and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation and relax the stomach muscles. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of these constituents aid in neutralizing excess stomach acid and promoting digestion. The piperine in Piper nigrum creams also stimulates the production of digestive enzymes, which helps to break down food and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity. Regular use of Piper nigrum creams may help to regulate stomach acid levels and provide relief from hyperacidity.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Collect 1 cup of fresh black pepper (Piper nigrum) seeds.
Dry the seeds in the sun for 2-3 days or using a low-heat oven (150°F - 200°F).
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried black pepper seeds into a fine powder.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the powder with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil or any other carrier oil in a bowl.
Store the mixture in a cool, dry place and apply 1-2 teaspoons as needed to help soothe hyperacidity.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains bioactive constituents like aloin, aloe-emodin, and mucilages that help neutralize excess stomach acid. Aloin has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, while aloe-emodin has antibacterial properties that fight off harmful bacteria that can contribute to hyperacidity. The mucilages in aloe vera creams form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, soothing and calming the digestive tract. This soothing effect helps to reduce acid production and alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and indigestion. By providing a protective barrier and reducing inflammation, aloe barbadensis creams can help to balance the body's digestive system and alleviate hyperacidity symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe barbadensis gel and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a bowl.
Heat the coconut oil in a pan over low heat until it melts.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter to the melted coconut oil. Stir until combined.
Pour the mixture into a separate bowl with the Aloe gel and mix well until smooth.
Pour the mixture into a container, let it cool, and store in the fridge for up to 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains active constituents such as aloin and aloe-emodin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation caused by hyperacidity. The gel of the aloe vera plant also contains vitamins A, C, and E, as well as beta-carotene, which act as antioxidants that neutralize free radicals and protect the stomach lining from damage. Aloe vera has mucilaginous properties, which create a protective barrier on the stomach lining, preventing acid from irritating the tissues. This barrier also helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the stomach lining, making it a suitable remedy for hyperacidity. Regular use of aloe vera creams can help alleviate symptoms of hyperacidity, such as heartburn and acid reflux.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for hyperacidity
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt the coconut oil and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Add the beeswax to the melted mixture and stir until it's fully dissolved.
Add the aloe vera gel, vitamin E oil, and stir well to combine. Remove from heat and let cool.
Once the mixture has cooled and thickened, pour it into a container and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before using.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help with hyperacidity. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have natural properties that can soothe the stomach and reduce inflammation. They can also help slow down digestion, giving the stomach time to process food without becoming too acidic. This can bring relief from heartburn and discomfort.
faq_safe: While herbal teas can provide relief from hyperacidity, consuming them every day may not be entirely safe. Some herbs like peppermint and ginger can cause stomach upset if overused. It's also possible to develop an intolerance to certain ingredients. Moderation is key to enjoying herbal teas without any issues.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can start to help with hyperacidity within a few days to a week. Some people notice a difference in their symptoms after drinking a cup of tea a day. The effect can be gradual, with the body slowly adjusting to the calming properties of the herbs.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for hyperacidity is best after meals. This helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce discomfort. Try sipping tea 30 minutes to an hour after eating to see if it brings relief. Some herbal teas, like peppermint or chamomile, can help calm the stomach and provide quick relief.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can affect how your body handles stomach acid. They might make conditions like hyperacidity worse or interact with medications meant to control it. If you're taking prescription meds for hyperacidity, you should be cautious when drinking herbal teas, as they could change how these medications work.
faq_pregnant: It's generally not recommended to consume herbal teas for hyperacidity during pregnancy. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and ginger, can stimulate digestion and relieve heartburn, but others can be too strong or cause allergic reactions. If you're experiencing heartburn, try sticking to water and gentle, pregnancy-friendly foods.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can help with hyperacidity, but others might not be suitable. If you're concerned, look for teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding. Some herbs, like sage and licorice root, may not be a good choice, so do some research before trying them.
